title: rusbyella pusilla
intro: Rusbyella pusilla is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Epacridaceae, native to the tropical mountains of New Guinea and nearby islands. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Rusbyella pusilla include its potential to alleviate fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. Rusbyella pusilla is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are prepared by steeping the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
